claim: Bitcoin being above $70,000 on the relevant April 20 Binance noon ET 1-minute close is still the clear base case, but the market appears slightly too confident because this contract resolves on one exact Binance BTC/USDT minute close rather than on broad spot strength or an intraday touch.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: small residual UI-versus-API settlement-surface ambiguity on Binance close capture
independently_verified_points: Polymarket rules explicitly resolve on Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute 12:00 ET final Close
The contract is exact-minute close based, not touch-based or broad daily pricing
All five personas independently converged on high-Yes but below-market estimates
Current BTC context at research time was materially above 70000, leaving a real but not decisive cushion
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: The main remaining gap is lack of a clean synthesis-stage direct capture of the exact Binance governing surface that will be used near settlement.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: A fast risk-off move or venue-specific weakness could still push the exact Apr 20 noon ET Binance close to 70000 or below despite BTC staying broadly strong.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: The main disagreement is how large a discount to apply for exact-minute close fragility versus the current price cushion.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle on Apr 20 has a final Close strictly above 70000.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: BTC short-horizon volatility into the Apr 20 12:00 ET Binance settlement minute
decision_blockers: Short-dated BTC volatility could erase the current cushion before the exact resolving minute
Exact governing Binance surface should ideally be rechecked near settlement
Any new macro or crypto-specific downside shock could compress fair value quickly
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Recheck Binance BTC/USDT and Polymarket pricing on Apr 19-20 ahead of the 12:00 ET settlement minute.

sections.Alpha summary: Market implied probability is 0.895. My post-synthesis range is 0.85-0.89 Yes. That is a marginal-to-moderate lean below market, not a large actionable dislocation. The likely mispricing is that traders are slightly underweighting exact-minute-close path risk because BTC is already comfortably above 70,000.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. No personas were missing. I checked the raw persona findings against the sidecars; the sidecars appeared broadly faithful and not materially distorted. Supporting assumption/evidence artifacts were referenced only indirectly through the raw findings. Coverage is complete because all intended lanes were present and converged on the same core mechanism/risk structure.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The synthesis baseline is the assignment market-implied probability of 0.895 Yes, with multiple lanes also noting live Polymarket page pricing in the low-90s. That baseline is directionally consistent with the swarm, but the swarm center around 0.86 treated the market as slightly too rich for an exact-minute close contract.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: My final estimate is 0.85 to 0.89 Yes. This keeps Yes clearly favored because BTC was materially above 70,000 at research time and only a few days remained, but it preserves a meaningful tail for a sub-70k exact close on the governing Binance minute.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: There is no material difference from the swarm-implied center of about 0.86. After reviewing the raw findings and doing a bounded verification pass, I did not find strong enough new evidence to move materially above or below the swarm baseline. The synthesis-stage review mainly confirmed that the right adjustment is a modest discount for exact-minute-close fragility, not a more aggressive bearish revision.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I modestly disagree with the market. The market's high-Yes framing is directionally right, but the final confidence still looks a bit rich because the contract is narrower than the headline 'Bitcoin above 70k on April 20' suggests. The key issue is not broad BTC strength; it is survival to one exact Binance noon ET close.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Independent verification was medium quality, not high. I independently checked the Polymarket contract wording via fresh fetch and confirmed the core mechanism: Binance BTC/USDT, 1-minute candle, 12:00 ET, final Close, strict bracket logic. I also compared the sidecars against the raw persona findings and found strong internal consistency across lanes. What remains weaker is synthesis-stage direct verification of the exact Binance governing display surface near settlement; one fresh Binance fetch attempt did not cleanly extract usable content. Because the final edge versus market is small and below-market rather than extreme, medium-quality verification is adequate for downstream caution but not for high-confidence edge claims.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: No. I did not compress materially toward the market because the swarm's below-market view was already modest and was independently supported by the rules-based mechanism review. If anything, the verification pass supported keeping the swarm slightly below market rather than moving up to match the richer live market prints.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key checkpoint is the Apr 20 12:00 ET Binance settlement minute, with the highest-value refresh in the final 24 hours before then. Time decay helps Yes if BTC remains comfortably above 70,000, but this edge can compress quickly if BTC trades back toward the low 70s. Waiting may improve the decision only if it provides a cleaner read on threshold proximity without losing too much entry value.
sections.Decision blockers: There are no major contract blockers; rules are fairly explicit. The practical blockers are ordinary short-horizon BTC volatility, the narrow exact-minute settlement mechanic, and the desirability of a final governing-surface recheck near settlement. This is not a blocked case so much as a high-probability case with limited edge and meaningful path dependence.
sections.Implication for the question: The question should currently be interpreted as Yes-favored, but not lock-level. A broad BTC-bullish regime is supportive, yet the actual contract can still fail on one badly timed drawdown at the exact governing Binance minute.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed that Yes is favored. All agreed the governing mechanism is the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute final Close on Apr 20. All agreed current price context put BTC materially above 70,000. All agreed the market is at least slightly too confident because this is an exact-minute close contract rather than a touch or broad-window condition.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: The main disagreement was weighting-based and timing-based rather than factual. Catalyst-hunter was the most bullish at 0.91, giving more weight to the current cushion and absence of obvious downside catalysts. Variant-view was the most cautious at 0.85, emphasizing exact-minute fragility and residual settlement-surface implementation risk. The rest clustered around 0.86. There was no meaningful factual disagreement about rules or current setup; the dispute was mainly over how much probability mass to assign to a late drawdown.
sections.Best countercase: The strongest surviving countercase, best represented by variant-view and risk-manager, is that the market is mentally rounding this into a generic 'BTC stays above 70k' proposition while underpricing the possibility of a sharp downside move or venue-specific weakness hitting the exact Apr 20 noon ET Binance close.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: BTC remains in an above-70k regime into Apr 20; Binance remains a clean governing venue; no major negative macro or crypto-specific shock arrives before settlement. Contested assumptions: how informative the current 6-7% cushion really is over a five-day horizon; how much exact-minute path risk deserves discounting. Fragile assumptions: Binance UI/API parity remains operationally clean enough that settlement-surface ambiguity stays immaterial.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: explicit Polymarket rules naming the exact Binance mechanism; multiple persona checks showing BTC materially above 70,000 at research time; short time remaining to settlement. Strongest contradictory evidence: BTC can move more than the current cushion over several days, and only one exact minute matters. Authoritative source-of-truth evidence: Polymarket rules and Binance-defined settlement venue. Ambiguous evidence: live market pricing looked even richer than the assignment snapshot, but that does not itself prove fair value.
sections.Evidence weighting: Most weight went to the governing contract mechanics and the observed cushion above 70,000. I downweighted broad market optimism that did not directly address the exact-minute-close structure. I also downweighted any lane confidence that leaned too heavily on current spot without enough allowance for short-horizon BTC volatility.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence is structural rather than narrative: this is a single exact future close market in a volatile asset. BTC does not need to collapse structurally for No; it only needs to be at or below 70,000 on the relevant Binance minute. That leaves a real tail risk even if the overall regime stays constructive.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: The resolution mechanics are mostly clear. Yes requires the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle for 12:00 ET on Apr 20 to have a final Close strictly above 70,000. Other venues, other times, intraminute highs, or broad daily pricing do not count. The only residual ambiguity is operational: the rules reference the Binance trading surface, while some verification paths used API proxies rather than the exact UI display. That is a minor implementation ambiguity, not a major conceptual one.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: This could create alpha only if the market is truly underpricing exact-minute path risk. But because the current gap versus market is small and the swarm does not show a large divergent view, the likely alpha is modest. The main destroyer of alpha here is overinterpreting current BTC strength as near-certainty in a narrow close-based contract.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A move toward or below the low-70k area before Apr 20 would push me materially lower. Conversely, if BTC remains comfortably above 70,000 into Apr 19-20 and a direct Binance governing-surface recheck is clean, I would move closer to the richer market view. Any new macro, regulatory, or crypto-specific shock that increases downside tail risk would also change the view.
sections.Highest-value next research: Single best next check: directly verify the Binance BTC/USDT governing 12:00 ET 1-minute candle surface close to settlement, while also checking whether BTC still retains a meaningful cushion above 70,000.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class was high-quality governing rules from Polymarket plus direct exchange-context checks from Binance in the upstream lanes. The most important secondary source class was independent contextual BTC price reporting. Evidence independence was medium: the rules source is distinct, but much of the context still depends on the same underlying BTC market state. Source-of-truth ambiguity is low-to-medium, mainly because of the exact Binance settlement-surface implementation detail. The synthesis is not badly bottlenecked by a single weak lane; the bigger limitation is that the extra synthesis-stage verification did not cleanly improve on the already-good upstream source capture.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, additional verification beyond lane summaries was used. Fresh synthesis-stage Polymarket fetch confirmed the exact rules wording and bracket logic. Cross-lane comparison materially increased confidence that the sidecars were faithful and that the swarm consensus was real rather than artifact-driven. It also exposed a mild provenance limitation: direct synthesis-stage Binance surface capture was not clean, so the synthesis did not earn a higher-confidence verification grade.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — strongest framing that current cushion and recent above-70k regime make Yes favored, but not as strongly as a touch-market intuition would imply. catalyst-hunter — best articulation of catalyst structure: the main live risk is absence or presence of a downside shock before the resolving minute. market-implied — best anchor on treating 0.895 as a serious prior while still discounting for mechanism narrowness. risk-manager — strongest statement of exact-minute path risk and why a ~7% cushion is substantial but not decisive. variant-view — clearest preservation of the minority countercase that the real issue is timestamp fragility and minor settlement-surface implementation risk, not broad BTC bearishness.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: exact-minute close crypto contracts often deserve a modest discount relative to generic threshold intuition even when spot is comfortably beyond the strike. Possible underbuilt driver: exact-resolution-minute risk / threshold proximity may deserve cleaner structured handling across repeated crypto threshold cases. Possible source-quality lesson: direct governing-surface proof capture remains valuable even when contract wording is already clear. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: yes; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: no; review later for swarm-method issue: no. Reason: repeated crypto threshold-close cases may benefit from a standardized way to encode exact-resolution-minute risk and threshold-proximity discounting.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Request decision-maker review only if action depends on extracting a small below-market edge now; otherwise wait for a final refresh closer to Apr 19-20. Best follow-up is not rerunning the whole swarm, but performing a targeted late check on Binance BTC/USDT threshold proximity and the exact governing settlement surface.
